result.summary: boxyhq.com currently fronts Ory docs: I found one documented developer HTTP surface for self-hosted Ory Polis (`/api/v1` with `Authorization: Api-Key`), one public MCP server (`https://ory-docs.mcp.kapa.ai`), and the Ory CLI authenticated either by `ory auth` browser login or workspace/project API keys.

polis_api_key.label: Ory Polis API key
polis_api_key.setup: For self-hosted Ory Polis, define one or more API keys in the service configuration via the `API_KEYS` environment variable as documented in [Environment Variables for Enterprise SSO](https://boxyhq.com/docs/polis/deploy/env-variables). Requests to `/api/v1/` must then send one of those keys in the `Authorization` header as `Api-Key <key>`.

ory_cli_login.label: Ory CLI browser login session
ory_cli_login.setup: Install the CLI following [Installation](https://www.ory.com/docs/guides/cli/installation), then run `ory auth` to open a browser and sign in to your Ory account as documented in [Basic functionality](https://www.ory.com/docs/guides/cli/cli-basics). This creates the local CLI auth session used to manage workspaces, projects, and project data.

ory_workspace_api_key.label: Ory workspace API key
ory_workspace_api_key.setup: Create an Ory workspace/project in the Ory console, then generate a workspace API key for automation. The CLI docs state these keys can be supplied via `ORY_WORKSPACE_API_KEY` for CI/CD and workspace-level management in [Basic functionality](https://www.ory.com/docs/guides/cli/cli-basics).

ory_project_api_key.label: Ory project API key
ory_project_api_key.setup: Create an Ory project in the Ory console, then generate a project API key for automation. The CLI docs state these keys can be supplied via `ORY_PROJECT_API_KEY` for CI/CD and project-data management in [Basic functionality](https://www.ory.com/docs/guides/cli/cli-basics).
